##NAMES=Petersenite-(Ce)
##RRUFFID=R120035
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Na_4_Ce_2_(CO_3_)_5_
##LOCALITY=Mont Saint-Hilaire, Rouville RCM, Montérégie, Québec, Canada
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Donald Doell
##DESCRIPTION=Pale yellow or pink (depending on light source) hexagonal prismatic crystals, associated with albite and aegirine.
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R120035
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(Na_2.92_K_0.03_Ca_0.58_Ba_0.24_Sr_0.22_)(Ce_1.07_La_0.51_Nd_0.31_Pr_0.12_)(CO_3_)_5_
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 20.945 b: 6.3989 c: 10.595 alpha: 90 beta: 120.37 gamma: 90 volume: 1225.1 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=780
